feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: At what point is a collection of notes a chord regarding their timing? I can hea…
entry.content: At what point is a collection of notes a chord regarding their timing? I can hear a rolled chord and hear the unity as if it were played all at once, many music chord libraries will play itself rolled to emphasize the individual notes while at the same time letting you hear it as a collective chord. Even in arpeggios if played quick enough I can "feel" the chords. Is there some threshold or agreed upon speed that my ears will discern a chord vs just playing parts of a scale?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Help for a noobie
entry.content: Been getting into Linux now and got the crazy idea of making a machine for general doc and sheet making and casual gaming (single player steam shit that doesn't need high end hardware). I've been checking linux user communities and got interested in making a pc with scrap parts that i can optimize for that end. Would love some pointers or some links to materials that i can read about older hardware that i can get cheap and make work. It's all a bit ovewhelming so if anybody got some tips as in w

entry.title: /g/ Technology: What the fuck did they do to Visual Studio?
entry.content: Ever since VS2012 when they raped the UI, it seems to only have gotten worse, not better. >vs2015 finally adopts C99 >vs2017 is a nothing burger, drops XP in compiler for No Fucking Reason >vs2019 is the last tolerable version since 2012 ruined the UI >vs2022 has AI slop, is slow as balls, dropped Vista support for compiler >vs2026 has More AI slop, is slow as balls, dropped 32 bit targets, dropped pre-10 support for both the editor and the compiler, ruined the one good theme (Blue), and somehow

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Ok, no bullshit. How good is currently AI at novel coding tasks? As in, not some…

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Lisa su, if you are here, save PC gaming!
entry.content: All you have to do is make an IO die for AM4 that is compatible with the Zen5 chiplets. In the AMD processors, the chiplets do the heavy work. The IO die does memory interface. So all the stuff DDR5 or DDR4 related, is done in the IO die. I bet if they wanted they could make an IO die or AM4 compatible with Zen5.

entry.title: /b/ Random: Fuck this world
entry.content: Guys, for years I’ve been looking into political theories on how to save Europe and go "trad" for nothing. The thing is, the people who’ve been bullying me are the ones who repost traditionalist videos but aren't actually traditional in their private lives they’re degenerate and even bully their own countrymen. WTF, guys you wouldn't believe how demotivating that is.

entry.title: /b/ Random: We should normalize aunts letting their nephews fuck them when the nephews get o…
entry.content: We should normalize aunts letting their nephews fuck them when the nephews get old enough. Sex is a sensitive thing, and many young men get hurt because of it. Something so special should be a good experience. Aunts, by blood or marriage, can be a safe place for nephews to explore their sexuality, learn, and practice. Nephews would know they can trust their aunts to take care of them. Plus, the aunt's experience would make the nephew a better lover when he does venture into other pussy. It could

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Megadeth's career was the poor man's copy of Metallica's
entry.content: >a bunch of kino albums everybody likes >then they sold out, starting with Youthanasia and ending with that abomination Risk >made an unexpected comeback with Endgame, their Death Magnetic >after that, turd after turd not an exact copy, because Metallica went from the sellout era (black album to St Anger) straight to Death Magnetic, while MD released 3 mediocre albums inbetween, but close enough

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /flg/ - F-List General #8398 Hard Labor Edition Previous Thread: >>83646907 F-li…
entry.content: /flg/ - F-List General #8398 Hard Labor Edition Previous Thread: >>83646907 F-list is a website for people interested in erotic roleplaying, and creating character profiles to find like-minded partners with similar interests and fetishes to roleplay with. It features a webchat (which can be used for textual roleplaying), group forums and more kinks and depravity than you can memorize. The purpose of this thread is to serve as a place where roleplayers can freely share and advertise their profile

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: First look at indie superhero series CRESTAR AND THE KNIGHT STALLION
entry.content: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xKJ IWNmXJdY >In a Detroit teetering on the edge of collapse, mysterious, battle-worn vigilante Crestar (Ajmal Zaheer Ahmad) discovers a young Arab-American man, Ali (Abdallah Jasim), who has survived an explosion and possesses world-changing superhuman abilities. Crestar takes the reluctant Ali — who takes on the moniker "Knight Stallion" — under his wing to train him as a crime-fighting hero. However, the duo faces an ancient evil force, the Devil's Knight, who

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Air conditioning
entry.content: Thoughts on Midea PortaSplit? It's like a mini-split, except that it doesn't require drilling a hole, and uses this hose(?) instead that is put through the window, the installation bracket for the outdoor unit doesn't require drilling either. Plus it's actually designed for euro windows first and foremost it seems, they're selling in primarily in the EU right now, not in the US. Either way, what's the best alternative for a proper mini-split if you're not allowed to drill a hole and you don't ha

entry.title: /news/ News: Army reverses Flu Shot ban after soldiers start dying
entry.content: These soldiers are suffering because Trump is a weak faggot who hates his country. https://abcnews.com/Health/military -services-requiring-recruits-flu-sh ots-air-force/story?id=134126794 A flu outbreak at the Air Force's basic training hub in San Antonio is worsening, according to two sources familiar with the situation. As of Tuesday, at least 222 recruits at Lackland Air Force Base, part of Joint Base San Antonio, had been diagnosed with the flu and four had been hospitalized, the two people

entry.title: /g/ Technology: I have been using Windows since XP all the way to 11, never had a single issue, …
entry.content: I have been using Windows since XP all the way to 11, never had a single issue, every fresh install I just spend ~20min configuring everything and it persists that way even after feature updates. I have been trying GNU/+Linux since 2014, been maining it on my laptop since 2019, Arch flavour, never had a single issue what-so-ever nor did I have anything break. I have been using macOS since 2017, also, never had a singular issue with it. What is it that you people of /g/ do to break your Operating

entry.title: /g/ Technology: I hear AI is being used in automation a lot these days, stuff like Zapier, Make.…
entry.content: I hear AI is being used in automation a lot these days, stuff like Zapier, Make.com, IFTTT and others and the various services that they connect to (and other low-code/no-code stuff that was popular before the AI boom). But I can't really think of a use case beyond asking it for guidance. Is this where AI Agents are supposed to come in? I'm not sure that I'd trust them with access to databases, CRM stuff and so on. If there are any wagies working in this field, I'd love to hear about it.

entry.content: >Morning Wood Edition Fapping-focused AIslop thread for those who enjoy plapping to AI thread perfection. A thread for prompters, editors, requesters, gooners, AI pornmommies, PLAPpers, Stable Diffusion directors, NovelAI artists—anything and everything AIslop. No limits past site rules; use Catbox galleries and uploads if needed. Keep dumps to a minimum and don't be a derailing cunt, thanks. entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Holy shit. It took me SIX HOURS to fucking mod this game. I downloaded roughly 7…
entry.content: Holy shit. It took me SIX HOURS to fucking mod this game. I downloaded roughly 70 mods and only had to cut about ten of them. I would have never fucking been able to get it working without the help of AI. The "average" person cannot do modding without help. You literally have to fucking know how to code and fix scripts. I had to keep taking screenshots of the script/merge issues and ask Gemini how to fix each one, granted once you know how it's not super hard but still... Absolutely fucking reta
